name: Delphine Marchetti
bio: Prestige-drama queen who rewrites her own dialogue on set and is usually right.

name: Kwabena Osei-Hart
bio: Franchise lead with a handshake reputation; every crew he leaves throws him a party.

name: Rosalind Vey
bio: Tabloid magnet whose casting doubles your premiere numbers and triples your insurance.

name: Tobias Kranemeyer
bio: Stage-trained heavyweight who treats a network procedural like a Chekhov revival.

name: Amara Nwadike
bio: The rare lead who can carry a weepy Thursday and a Friday farce in one week.

name: Gustavo Pineiro
bio: Detective-show icon who now demands a producer credit and a personal trailer chef.

name: Minori Tachibana
bio: Voice work made her famous; live-action made her respected, and she never mentions either.

name: Cormac Brannigan
bio: Grizzled patriarch specialist who can make a weather report sound like a confession.

name: Priya Ramaswamy
bio: Streaming breakout who is one good showrunner away from being genuinely great.

name: Lennox Fairweather
bio: Handsome, hugely marketable, and physically incapable of delivering a subordinate clause.

name: Yolanda Birchwood
bio: Thirty years of flawless work and not one anecdote about her being difficult.

name: Dorian Eszterhazy
bio: Astonishing on camera, catastrophic off it; two showrunners have quit rather than continue.

name: Mabel Quintanilla
bio: Former primetime queen, still magnificent, now available for a startling discount.

name: Terrence Oyelaran
bio: Comedy lead with dramatic chops he keeps quietly auditioning for and not getting.

name: Solveig Lindqvist
bio: Nordic import whose stillness on screen makes other actors look like they're flailing.

name: Hollis Derringer
bio: Coasting on a hit he headlined in 2009 and reminding everyone hourly.

name: Chidinma Obiako
bio: Two seasons of soap boot camp produced a technician who never blows a take.

name: Rafferty McHale
bio: Sitcom dad in four failed pilots; the fifth one might finally be the hit.

name: Beatriz Calloway
bio: The precinct captain everyone believes; casting directors call her the credibility machine.

name: Anselm Verhoeff
bio: A living masterclass who now works cheap because nobody under fifty remembers him.

name: Jazmine O'Toole
bio: Reality-show alumna converting fame into acting work faster than she's converting talent.

name: Nikolai Svetlanov
bio: Plays villains so well that strangers apologize to him in supermarkets.

name: Lorraine Akiyama
bio: Six procedurals, six seasons each, never once late to a call time.

name: Bertrand Nsele
bio: Reliable second lead who quietly wonders when someone will let him be first.

name: Imogen Vasquez-Rowe
bio: Brilliant, brittle, and known to walk off a set over a badly written comma.

name: Desmond Achterberg
bio: Career character man with 340 credits and precisely zero enemies in the industry.

name: Soraya Benkirane
bio: Film-school discovery whose screen test made a room of executives go silent.

name: Walton Priddy
bio: Funny in the room, unbearable by week three, gone by episode nine.

name: Harriet Mbeki-Strand
bio: Soap veteran who can learn eleven pages overnight and make them sing.

name: Ignacio Delacruz-Bell
bio: Stunt-trained lead who does his own falls and negotiates his own contracts badly.

name: Philippa Ondaatje-Moss
bio: Critics adore her, audiences forget her, and she's made peace with exactly one of those.

name: Jerome Tinsdale
bio: Daytime legend convinced his primetime renaissance is one phone call away.

name: Nia Okonkwo-Pratt
bio: Improv-house standout with impeccable timing and a terrifying work ethic.

name: Anders Holmqvist
bio: Every network has cast him as a coroner; three of them cast him twice.

name: Leticia Sandoval-Ruiz
bio: Telenovela-trained and utterly unflappable; directors request her for the hardest scenes.

name: Corbin Featherstonhaugh
bio: Modelling-to-acting pipeline graduate whose agent talks considerably better than he acts.

name: Ayanda Mokoena-Frost
bio: Theatre royalty at home, gloriously unknown here, and priced accordingly.

name: Marisol Thackeray
bio: Forty years of daytime made her fast, sharp, and openly contemptuous of rehearsal.

name: Idris Vanderhoek
bio: Six years of tiny parts, one enormous monologue, and now a very busy inbox.

name: Georgina Pell
bio: Was almost famous twice and has an excellent, slightly bitter story about each.

name: Kelechi Adeyemi-Stone
bio: Conservatory graduate who treats a two-line guest spot like a career-defining role.

name: Vittorio Lamonte
bio: Magnificent when sober, and nobody will tell you when that reliably is.

name: Tallulah Brightman
bio: Physical comedian who broke a rib for a sight gag and called it worth it.

name: Hamish Oduya
bio: The guy from that ad, now quietly assembling a genuinely respectable resume.

name: Perpetua Nightingale
bio: Two golden-age classics on her CV and a mordant opinion about every one of you.

name: Sanjay Bhattacharya-Lowe
bio: Can play doctor, suspect, brother, or best friend, and has played all four this year.

name: Claudette Fournier-Ross
bio: Spent a decade raising kids; came back better and considerably cheaper than she should be.

name: Olamide Carrington
bio: Web-series famous, network-untested, and cheaper this month than she'll ever be again.

name: Gunther Alsleben
bio: Plays authority figures with a weariness no younger actor can convincingly fake.

name: Esperanza Villalobos
bio: An undiscovered gem: three festival awards, no agent worth the name, no budget line.

name: Rupert Caskey
bio: Fired from two series for the same argument about his character's motivation.

name: Zainab Halloran
bio: Nineteen when she shot her first pilot; already the most prepared person on set.

name: Barnaby Quist
bio: Sketch-show utility player who does forty voices and has never headlined anything.

name: Noor Shirazi-Kemp
bio: Every director who hires her tries to hire her again; nobody has told the marketing department.

name: Franklin Osgood-Hale
bio: Retired twice, unretired twice, and still better than most of your cast.

name: Suki Nakamura-Boyd
bio: Voice-acting prodigy trying to convince someone she has a face as well as a range.

name: Emeka Stanhope
bio: Dependable day-player who has died on screen in nine different franchises.

name: Annika Torvald
bio: Craft eighty, salary eleven thousand, and an agent who hasn't noticed the discrepancy.

name: Jean-Baptiste Okoro
bio: Twenty years of international arthouse; nobody here can pronounce his best film.

name: Petra Vandenbosch
bio: Once turned down a network lead on principle and has regretted it since Tuesday, 2011.

name: Caspian Morrow-Idowu
bio: The bargain of the decade if anyone bothers to watch his student film.

name: Marguerite Selby-Adeyinka
bio: Left law for acting at twenty-nine and hasn't wasted a single day since.

name: Orson Pettibone
bio: Blacklisted himself with one interview in 2014 and still won't take it back.

name: Leilani Fotu-Marsh
bio: Islander theatre circuit's brightest export, currently working a day job between auditions.

name: Dmitri Halversson
bio: Menacing in exactly one register, but nobody does that register better.

name: Constance Abioye
bio: Never had a break, never gave a bad performance, never asked for more money.

name: Tobiah Rennick
bio: Child-actor graduate navigating the awkward gap between cute and castable.

name: Valentina Krasniqi
bio: Speaks five languages, works in three, and is unknown in all of them.

name: Hugo Mbanefo-Clay
bio: Convinced his comedy is ahead of its time; it has been for twenty-two years.

name: Seraphine Dubois-Oyelowo
bio: Craft of a veteran, profile of a stranger, price of a background extra.

name: Ronan Fitzallan
bio: Fifty years of small screens and small rooms; still the best listener in any scene.

name: Jolanda Espiritu
bio: Four years of daytime under another name that she'd rather you didn't research.

name: Augustin Kyeremeh
bio: Twenty-four, eighty craft, nine thousand a week; someone is about to get very lucky.

name: Wilhelmina Oduro-Pike
bio: Ten years of background work and a folder of coverage nobody has ever watched.

name: Pascal Nwosu-Reed
bio: Raw, eager, and one good acting coach away from being genuinely worth something.

name: Harlan Vespucci
bio: Writes the episode everyone remembers, then disappears for eleven weeks without warning.

name: Junie Oyelaran-Park
bio: Punches up other people's scripts so well that three showrunners hide her from each other.

name: Desmond Agyeman-Fell
bio: Has broken 200 murders and can plot a season finale on a napkin in ten minutes.

name: Ines Carrasco-Bly
bio: Emotional-beat specialist whose table drafts routinely make grown executives cry.

name: Maxwell Tsuruoka
bio: Animation veteran who insists every joke be storyboarded before he'll admit it's funny.

name: Aoife Mbatha
bio: Two staff jobs, zero credits above the line, and an unproduced pilot people whisper about.

name: Gideon LaRosa
bio: Old-guard scribe who fights every note and wins roughly a third of the time.

name: Thandiwe Okorafor-Lin
bio: Can write any tone in any room and has never once missed a delivery date.

name: Bertram Oakhurst
bio: Wrote 140 episodes of network crime and will tell you which eleven were good.

name: Suheila Mansour-Day
bio: Fellowship graduate whose spec script has been passed around like contraband.

name: Rory Blackfoot-Nnamdi
bio: Structural obsessive who redesigns the whole season if episode three feels wrong.

name: Clementine Ferrars
bio: Twenty-year comedy room survivor with a legendary index of unused punchlines.

name: Obinna Strathmore
bio: His dialogue is untouchable; his outlines are a rumour he refuses to confirm.

name: Marisela Quintero-Vaughn
bio: Turns out sixty daytime scripts a year without ever repeating a cliffhanger.

name: Nils Oyelowo-Brandt
bio: Wrote a viral short at twenty-two and has been mysteriously unhired ever since.

name: Perpetua Lindgren
bio: Investigative journalist turned dramatist; her research notes are longer than her scripts.

name: Casimir Adeleke
bio: Solid pages, impossible meetings; two rooms have asked him to attend by phone.

name: Hyacinth Mbugua-Porter
bio: Fastest rewrite in the building and still officially credited as a writers' assistant.

name: Lorcan Devereux-Ito
bio: Anthology specialist who has never written a second episode about the same person.

name: Beatrix Hollingsworth-Ade
bio: Won everything in the nineties and now writes better than she did then, cheaper.

name: Tomas Relampago
bio: Unscripted's dirty secret: he scripts it, and he's very good at pretending otherwise.

name: Nkechi Vandermolen
bio: Stand-up background, surgical instincts, and a rate that hasn't caught up to either.

name: Alistair Pemberton-Nkosi
bio: Genuinely gifted and genuinely exhausting; his last room voted anonymously to replace him.

name: Willa Ferrante-Osei
bio: Four seasons of uncredited heavy lifting; the credited names all won awards.

name: Emmett Brackenridge
bio: Documentary narration craftsman who can make a fishing quota sound like a thriller.

name: Soraya Lindstrom-Bello
bio: Writes with a precision that makes veterans nervous and staffing agents oblivious.

name: Douglas Ainsworth-Eze
bio: Never written a great episode, never written a bad one, always delivers on Friday.

name: Ximena Oduya-Frank
bio: Specialist in the slow betrayal; her season-five twists get written about for years.

name: Fenwick Abubakar
bio: Believes multi-cam never died and will out-argue anyone who suggests otherwise.

name: Ottoline Mbeki-Vance
bio: Twenty-four, unrepresented, and quietly the best unhired writer in the city.

name: Balthazar Cruikshank
bio: Difficult, defensive, and occasionally produces a page you'd frame on the wall.

name: Celestine Nakagawa-Orr
bio: Writes children's television that adults quote back to her at parties.

name: Harold Osunde-Wray
bio: Newsroom lifer who writes cold opens that make anchors sound smarter than they are.

name: Ivory Belmonte-Sahni
bio: Genre-agnostic utility ace; three showrunners have tried to poach her this quarter.

name: Quentin Mabaso-Rill
bio: A genuine artist who has burned down two rooms and would happily burn a third.

name: Vivienne Arkwright-Dube
bio: Three consecutive prestige hits and a reputation for finishing under budget.

name: Marcus Oyinlola-Hale
bio: A bomb waiting to go off: staggering talent, zero impulse control, two abandoned seasons.

name: Junko Abernathy
bio: Runs the calmest comedy room in television and has never missed an airdate.

name: Ezekiel Tamboura
bio: Built two nine-season franchises and thinks serialized storytelling is a passing fad.

name: Lisbeth Okonjo-Frayne
bio: Youngest showrunner on the lot and the only one whose crew requests her by name.

name: Reginald Oduya-Mbeki
bio: Elder statesman of the serialized weepy, still capable of a genuinely lethal season finale.

name: Paloma Viridian-Cross
bio: Demands final cut, usually earns it, and has made two studio heads cry in a hallway.

name: Stellan Mworia
bio: First-time runner, second-in-command on three hits, and cheaper than his resume implies.

name: Georgiana Petrescu-Hall
bio: Unscripted architect who can format a hit franchise out of literally any premise.

name: Abdiel Ferreira-Nkemdirim
bio: Turns tired formats into critical darlings and refuses to explain how.

name: Honorata Blessing-Shaw
bio: Ran a cancelled masterpiece; the industry blames the network, the network blames her.

name: Thaddeus Onyekwere
bio: Ran four series, fired from two, and still gets meetings on reputation alone.

name: Cordelia Hasenberg
bio: Anthology purist who would rather run a small perfect show than a big loud one.

name: Malachy Sithole-Brandt
bio: Reliable machine for network hours; will never make art, will never miss a delivery.

name: Yumiko Oyelaran-Bray
bio: Animation runner whose shows outlive their networks and quietly print merchandising money.

name: Barnabas Eluwande
bio: Ran the biggest show of 1998 and now works for a fraction of what he's worth.

name: Nadia Corvalan-Pike
bio: Brilliant at the pilot, restless by episode six, gone before the finale twice now.

name: Osric Tembo-Langley
bio: Number two on a monster franchise; everyone knows he actually ran the last three seasons.

name: Iolanthe Mercado-Fisk
bio: Invented three formats now licensed in forty countries and still answers her own phone.

name: Rutger Nwachukwu-Samms
bio: Ran a web series into a cult phenomenon; has never managed a budget over six figures.

name: Philomena Adeyinka-Groves
bio: Visionary, uninsurable, and worth every catastrophic argument if the season lands.

name: Lucian Vasilescu-Mbeki
bio: Steady hand for troubled productions; the studio's designated adult in the room.

name: Berenice Okafor-Tallis
bio: Cheapest genuinely excellent comedy runner available, and her agent has no idea.

name: Aurelio Santangelo-Owusu
bio: Made four cult classics nobody watched and is entirely at peace about it.

name: Delilah Onwuachi-Vance
bio: Never run anything, writes like she's run everything, costs less than a staff writer.

name: Gideon Marchbanks-Esho
bio: The producer studios call when a show is on fire and the star has stopped speaking.

name: Rosamund Kettleby
bio: Has never once let a production overrun and is feared by every line producer alive.

name: Amadou Brightwater
bio: Unscripted deal-closer whose talent budgets always somehow include a private jet.

name: Seong-Hae Lindqvist
bio: Runs a set so efficiently that directors accuse her of bending the space-time continuum.

name: Cyprian Oduro-Veitch
bio: Old-school power producer who still thinks a screaming match counts as a note session.

name: Tabitha Nzeogwu-Frame
bio: Documentary background; secures impossible locations and impossible interviews for nothing.

name: Ferdinand Oyelaran-Crisp
bio: Comedy producer who protects his writers from the network and bills them for it later.

name: Marguerite Oyelowo-Stang
bio: Forty years of credits, a rolodex like a national archive, and semi-retired rates.

name: Kaveh Brannock
bio: Physical-production specialist who can shoot a car chase for the price of a dinner scene.

name: Antonella Quaye-Birch
bio: Coordinator promoted three years early and outperforming everyone who objected.

name: Lambert Nkosi-Hyde
bio: Two shows shut down on his watch and a remarkable talent for never being at fault.

name: Joelle Vanterpool-Osagie
bio: Every showrunner she's worked with has tried to bring her along to the next job.

name: Ravi Tindalls-Mbogo
bio: Twenty-six and already the person everyone actually asks when something breaks.

name: Eudora Fenwick-Adisa
bio: Kept a daytime institution on air for nineteen years on a budget that shouldn't have worked.

name: Anwar Pellington
bio: Format-flipper who has rebooted four dead franchises and killed one for good.

name: Sylvestre Mokwena-Hobbs
bio: Knows every guild rule by heart and uses roughly half of them in good faith.

name: Birdie O'Callaghan-Nnaji
bio: Made an award-winning docuseries on a credit card and hasn't been properly paid since.

name: Horatio Belyavsky
bio: Prestige producer with immaculate taste and an expense report nobody dares audit.

name: Lucinda Ashworth-Baptiste
bio: The quiet reason four different hit shows came in on time and on budget.

name: Osmund Tetteh-Vail
bio: Fresh out of a mailroom, already fixing problems two pay grades above him.

name: Esteban Krivosheev
bio: Pilot specialist whose openings sell shows before a single line is spoken.

name: Nomvula Hargreaves
bio: Shoots fast, shoots beautifully, and has never once run into golden time.

name: Pierrick Vandenberg-Alao
bio: Every frame is gorgeous and every schedule he touches slips by four days.

name: Harriet Osei-Lindenbaum
bio: Ninety network hours directed, zero drama caused, permanently underbooked for it.

name: Tarquin Mbugua-Sloane
bio: Music-video pedigree, cinematic instincts, and still learning what a coverage day means.

name: Annegret Solano-Pike
bio: Directed the era-defining finale of 2004 and has been affordable ever since.

name: Kwabena Thistlewood
bio: Multi-cam maestro who can land a live audience taping without a single reshoot.

name: Suraya Okpara-Mendel
bio: Two short films, one festival prize, and a fee that will not stay this low.

name: Gilbert Nakamura-Fontaine
bio: Journeyman with 200 episodes who describes his style as whatever the schedule allows.

name: Beatrice Oyelaran-Vashti
bio: Handheld naturalist whose actors say she gets performances out of nowhere.

name: Aurelian Boateng-Kerr
bio: Great once, difficult always, and now cheap enough that networks risk it anyway.

name: Minh-Thanh Okoli
bio: Animation director with an eye for staging that live-action people keep trying to steal.

name: Roderick Ansah-Plume
bio: Talented enough to keep getting hired, abrasive enough to never get hired twice.

name: Elowen Tshabalala-Reeve
bio: Shadowed six directors, out-directed four of them, and still bills like a trainee.

name: Bartholomew Nzuki-Frayn
bio: Golden-age craftsman who still blocks a scene better than anyone half his age.

name: Chastity Rambeau-Oduya
bio: Daytime empress whose endorsement can make a book, a brand, or a scandal.

name: Garrick Belanov
bio: Enormously popular, cannot read an autocue, and somehow that's part of the appeal.

name: Odalys Fenwright
bio: The interviewer politicians dread and everyone else begs their publicist to book.

name: Reuben Castellanos-Mbeki
bio: Makes a tired quiz format feel like a night out; twelve years and still fresh.

name: Lavinia Oduro-Sparks
bio: Social-media giant, live-television liability, and worth the ratings risk on a good week.

name: Emeka Lindhoffer
bio: Thirty years of unimpeachable credibility going for a fraction of a reality star's fee.

name: Perpetual Mwangi-Drake
bio: Radio-honed timing and warmth; one prime slot away from being genuinely expensive.

name: Stanislav Oyibo-Crane
bio: Hosted a national institution for eighteen years and still resents being replaced.

name: Tuppence Adekoya-Vale
bio: Kids-TV natural who can hold a live studio of nine-year-olds with a single eyebrow.

name: Ferdinand Oyelude-Marsh
bio: Best presenter nobody has heard of, currently narrating corporate videos for rent money.
